channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Kickstarter isn’t just for indie passion projects anymore
item.description: Despite a triumphant world premiere at Cannes last May, the politically unsparing Donald Trump biopic The Apprentice was stuck in cinematic limbo.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: SpaceX flight launches 4 space tourists into first-ever polar orbit
item.description: A bitcoin investor who bought a SpaceX flight for himself and three polar explorers blasted off Monday night on the first rocket ride to carry people over the North and South poles. Chun Wang, a Chinese-born entrepreneur, hurtled into orbit from NASA’s Kennedy Space Center.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Filming Begins on House of the Dragon Season 3 Amidst Spat with George R.R. Martin
item.description: House of the Dragon season 3 filming has begun, overshadowed by a dispute between showrunner Ryan Condal and author George R.R. Martin over creative decisions.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Detroit production of Mozart opera turns its female characters into automatons
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Audiences at the Detroit Opera House expecting their performance of “Cosi fan tutte" to begin with the overture may be surprised to hear instead a product launch from a tech company CEO.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SILVERMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nintendo Switch 2 Direct live build-up: the start time, live countdown, latest news, rumors, and our predictions before the big show
item.description: The Nintendo Switch 2 Direct is just a day away! Join us for everything you need to know ahead of the event.
item.dc:creator: dash.wood@futurenet.com (Dashiell Wood), TechRadar

item.title: ChatGPT 4o’s image generation is now free for everyone – 3 ways to use the new AI tool without following the Studio Ghibli herd
item.description: ChatGPT 4o's image generation is now free for everyone to try. Here are 3 ways you can use the impressive new AI tool.
item.dc:creator: john-anthony.disotto@futurenet.com (John-Anthony Disotto), TechRadar

item.title: AI agents can (and will) be scammed
item.description: Generative AI’s newest super stars — independent-acting agents — are on a tear. Organizations are adopting the technology at a staggering rate because they can use APIs or be embedded with standard apps and automate all kinds of business processes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI Productivity Shift: Amazon, OpenAI, and Zhipu AI Revolutionize Digital Services
item.description: Amazon's Nova Act, OpenAI's open-weight language model, and Zhipu AI's free assistant are changing the game for businesses and consumers." char="160
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Vecttor automatiza su flota con una solución de llave digital
item.description: Vecttor, filial del grupo Cabify, da un paso más en su apuesta por la innovación y digitalización junto a Telefónica Tech y la empresa de soluciones de transporte conectado Geotab.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Elon Musk says he sent MAGA influencer Ashley St. Clair $2.5 million, open to paternity test
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk and conservative influencer Ashley St. Clair publicly feuded on the social platform X on Monday after she accused the world’s richest person of withdrawing child care support and refusing to take a paternity test.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Apple faces a $162 million fine from French regulators. Here’s why
item.description: Apple was hit with a 150 million euro ($162.4 million) fine by French antitrust regulators on Monday for abusing its dominant position in mobile app advertising on its devices via a privacy control tool.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: U.S. seeks to ramp up social media data collection. Here’s what to know about Homeland Security’s plans
item.description: U.S. immigration officials are asking the public and federal agencies to comment on a proposal to collect social media handles from people applying for benefits such as green cards or citizenship, to comply with an executive order from President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: NASA's newly returned astronauts say they would fly on Boeing's Starliner capsule again
item.description: NASA's celebrity astronauts Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams say they would fly on Boeing's Starliner capsule again.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Apple’s Swift language gets version manager
item.description: Apple has introduced swiftly 1.0, a version manager for the Swift programming language that is intended to ease the process of installing, managing, and updating the user’s Swift tool chain. Swiftly 1.0 was announced March 28.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Tesla protests across Bay Area ignite Trump resistance, but intra-party strife ahead
item.description: After almost stunned paralysis among Democrats in the early weeks of Trump’s onslaught of executive orders, the Trump resistance is gaining traction.
item.dc:creator: Julia Prodis Sulek, The Mercury News

item.title: Los Angeles sheriff's department to retest 4,000 DNA samples after using faulty kits for 8 months
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles County Sheriff’s Department said this week it will retest 4,000 DNA samples and open an internal investigation after learning that it used potentially flawed test kits for eight months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Figure skating embraces the future with new technology, modern concepts to engage athletes and fans
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ashley Wagner and Ben Agosto know what it's like to step off the ice at the figure skating world championships and feel as though they've conquered the sport. And what it feels like when the notoriously fickle sport bites back.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Microsoft abandons data center projects as OpenAI considers its own, hinting at a market shift
item.description: OpenAI is reportedly contemplating building its own data center as Microsoft backs off on its own infrastructure investments. OpenAI has privately discussed building and operating its first data center to house storage, which is essential for developing sophisticated AI models.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Judge allows newspaper copyright lawsuit against OpenAI to proceed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has ruled that The New York Times and other newspapers can proceed with a copyright lawsuit against OpenAI and Microsoft seeking to end the practice of using their stories to train artificial intelligence chatbots. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Teams successful on 52.2% of ball/strike challenges during spring training robot umpire test
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Teams won 52.2% of their ball/strike challenges during the spring training test of robot umpires, and catchers had a markedly better success rate than pitchers.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Red Hat’s AI portfolio evolves to address enterprise deployment challenges
item.description: Red Hat announced updates to Red Hat OpenShift AI and Red Hat Enterprise Linux AI (RHEL AI), with a goal of addressing the high costs and technical complexity of scaling AI beyond pilot projects into full deployment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: FBI warns about document conversion sites spreading malware
item.description: The FBI warns that some free online document converters load malware onto people's computers, making their private information vulnerable to attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Napster sold to tech commerce company for $207 million
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A brand that was notoriously connected to music piracy before reemerging as a subscription music service has been sold to Infinite Reality for $207 million.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
